data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Stocks Gain 3.9 Million Barrels in Week Ended June 27, EIA Reports
data.description:
U.S. crude oil inventories grew by 3.9 million barrels to 419 million barrels in the week ended June 27, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its This Week in Petroleum report released on Thursday.

The increase came as a surprise as analysts in a Reuters poll had forecast a decline of 1.8 million barrels.

Crude oil production gained 6,000 barrels per day to 13.43 million b/d, the EIA reported. Crude oil imports also rose 144,000 b/d to 6.14 million b/d but crude oil exports dropped 400,000 b/d to 3.56 million b/d, according to the agency.

Total gasoline stocks increased by 4.2 million barrels to 232.1 million barrels. Distillate stocks, however, fell 1.7 million barrels to 103.6 million barrels, the EIA reported.

data.title: Singapore Shares Muted as Manufacturing Activity Edges Up in June; Addvalue Technologies Up 13%
data.description:
Singapore shares closed marginally higher on Thursday, maintaining to 4,000 threshold, as the city-state reported marginal improvement in its manufacturing activity for June, mirroring mixed gains in the region ahead of key US jobs data.

The Straits Times Index (STI), a key benchmark for the Singapore Exchange, ranged between 4,001.84 and 4,019.57 throughout the day. It ended the session at 4,019.57, up 8.80 points or 0.22% compared to Wednesday's close.

In economic news, Singapore's manufacturing activity, measured by the purchasing manager's index (PMI), was up slightly to 50 points in June from 49.7 points in May, according to a report by the Singapore Institute of Purchasing and Materials Management (SIPMM).

In company news, shares of Addvalue Technologies were over 13% at the close with the company gaining new orders worth about $1.5 million for its software defined radio modules.

Shanaya was up nearly 8% after the company received a consent letter from JTC for the proposed disposal of its Kian Tech facility in Singapore.

Meanwhile, Sanli Environmental entered into a placement agreement for up to 33,333,333 shares at SG$0.12 per share to raise up to SG$4 million.

data.title: Cyprus Consumer Prices Drop the Most Since 2021
data.description:
Consumer prices in Cyprus declined by 0.4% year-on-year in June 2025, following a 0.2% decrease in the previous month.

This marked the second consecutive month of deflation and the steepest fall in consumer prices since March 2021.

Prices mostly dropped for food and non-alcoholic beverages (-0.9% vs 2.0% in May), housing and utilities (-2.2% vs -1.5%), and furnishings, household equipment and supplies (-0.9% vs -0.5%).

Meanwhile, costs rose for alcoholic beverages and tobacco (1.0% vs 0.9%), recreation and culture (5.1% vs 4%), and restaurants and hotels (5.5% vs 4.9%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices fell by 0.2% in June, reversing a 0.2% rise in May.

data.title: Tripadvisor Stock Jumps as Activist Starboard Takes Stake. Why It's Good for the Travel Sector. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Callum Keown

Tripadvisor stock surged in Thursday's premarket, on course for a second day of big gains following a report that activist investor Starboard Value has taken a stake of more than 9% in the travel review company.

The investment is worth around $160 million, as of Wednesday's closing prices, and comes after the travel company rejected several takeover offers in the past year, The Wall Street Journal reported. Tripadvisor and Starboard did not immediately respond to a request for comment Thursday.

Shares of Tripadvisor, which has a market capitalization of just below $2 billion, pointed 4% higher in premarket trading after rallying 8.1% Wednesday. The stock is still down 15% over the past 12 months.

However, the company's shares are now up 1.5% in 2025 through Wednesday's close, joining its larger rivals Airbnb and Booking Holdings in positive territory for the year. Online travel agent Expedia, however, is down 8% this year.

It has been a tumultuous year for travel stocks, which were hit hard at times in the first half due to the macro uncertainty stemming from President Donald Trump's tariffs agenda. But the sector has recovered strongly from its lows in April.

While the activist investor's plans aren't yet clear, Starboard's confidence in Tripadvisor could be a sign that the sector's comeback can continue in the second half.

Write to Callum Keown at callum.keown@dowjones.com

data.title: Malaysian Shares Snap Winning Streak on Profit-Taking Activities; Tenaga Nasional Shares Drop 4%
data.description:
Malaysian stocks ended in the red zone on Tuesday, paring gains from the previous sessions on profit taking. The investor sentiment was dull amid mixed performance by regional peers.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, shed 1.22 points, to end 0.08% lower at 1,548.99. The day range was between 1,540.35 and 1,548.99.

In corporate news, A1 AK Koh Group's initial public offering was oversubscribed by 1.11 times, ahead of its ACE Market debut on Bursa Malaysia on July 11. The processed food and beverage maker attracted 2,289 applications from the Malaysian public for 46.8 million shares valued at 11.7 million ringgit.

Shares of Tenaga Nasional dropped about 4% on Thursday's close after the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia appealed a disputed additional tax bill for 2018.

Shares of Theta Edge slid marginally after it proposed a private placement of up to 23.6 million shares to third-party investors. The shares represent 20% of the company's enlarged issued share capital.

data.title: Stocks at record high ahead of US payrolls, UK bonds steady
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dollar steady, bond yields slip before US payrolls
  • Investors await passage of US tax cut and spending bill
  • UK bond markets stabilise after sharp selloff

World shares were at a record high and the dollar firmed on Thursday ahead of key U.S. jobs data, while Europe saw Britain's bond markets steady after debt worries prompted a selloff the previous session.

A deal between the United States and Vietnam ahead of next week's worldwide U.S. trade tariff deadline provided another boost for the bulls overnight after a hectic run up in markets in recent weeks.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index advanced 0.4%, leaving MSCI's main 47-country world shares gauge EURONEXT:IACWI at its seventh record high in the last eight sessions and the dollar close to ending a four-day run of falls.

Britain's bonds clawed back some of their heavy losses spurred a day earlier by uncertainty over finance minister Rachel Reeves' future, but they remained weaker than recent levels.

The 20-year gilt yield (GB20YT=RR), which is a proxy of longer term UK government borrowing costs, eased by 8 basis points after its biggest spike on Wednesday since October 2022 - during the ill-fated premiership of Liz Truss.

A tearful appearance from UK finance minister Rachel Reeves in parliament had sparked uncertainty about her future and Britain's public finances after the government was forced to ditch billions of pounds worth of welfare spending cuts.

"Some worries remain about the government being backed into a corner and losing its grip on the public finances," said Susannah Streeter, head of money and markets at broker Hargreaves Lansdown.

Focus was also on the U.S. payrolls report later which will steer ongoing debate on if and when the Federal Reserve will next cut U.S. interest rates.

Analysts are forecasting a rise of 110,000 jobs in June with the jobless rate ticking up to 4.3% but the stakes are high after a private sector payrolls report surprised with the first fall in over two years.

Traders were also waiting to see if U.S. President Donald Trump's tax and spending bill gets passed by Congress in the next 24 hours. The bill is expected to add $3.3 trillion to the United States' $36 trillion of national debt over the next decade, according to nonpartisan analysts.

Wall Street had closed on Wednesday at record highs after Trump announced that the U.S. had struck a trade deal with Vietnam, including a 20% tariff on exports to the U.S. That is lower than the 46% tariff that had been threatened, but still much higher than previous rates.

Vietnamese shares gained 0.5% to the highest since April 2022 although the local dong currency hit a record low of 26,229 per dollar.

"More trade deals may soon be announced but the 20% tariff agreed with Vietnam does not augur well, and that or even higher could become the norm for some including Europe and Japan," said Shane Oliver, chief economist at AMP.

Indeed, Japan has invoked national interests as talks with the U.S. struggled, while South Korean President Lee Jae Myung said on Thursday U.S. tariff negotiations were looking difficult and he could not say if talks could conclude by next Tuesday.

MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares (.MIAP00000PUS) closed up 0.3% higher. China's blue-chip index rose 0.6% after limp services data bolstered expectations of more stimulus, while Japan's Nikkei finished flat.

Nasdaq futures and S&P 500 futures were broadly flat in Europe too.

JOBS RISK

The dollar was hovering just above a three-year low against a basket of its major peers, up 0.1% for the day. Sterling (GBP=EBS) recovered 0.2% to $1.3662 after its 0.8% fall on Wednesday.

The main risk event for markets will be the U.S. payrolls figures.

The resilience of the labour market is a major reason the majority of Federal Reserve members say they can afford to hold off on cutting rates until they can gauge the real impact of tariffs on inflation.

"These labour market indicators warn of the risk that the unemployment rate could spike to 4.4%, the highest since October 2021," said Tony Sycamore, analyst at IG. "This would quickly increase the probability of a July Fed rate cut to around 70%."

Futures imply just a 25% probability for a rate cut this month from the Fed, which has not eased policy at all this year, drawing the ire of Trump who reiterated his call on Wednesday for Chair Jerome Powell to resign.

Trump, who said rates should be cut to 1% from the current Fed benchmark of 4.25% to 4.50%, has repeatedly railed against Powell for not lowering borrowing costs since his return to the White House in January.

A UBS survey on Thursday showed two in three reserve managers fear Fed independence is at risk and nearly half think the rule of law in the U.S. may deteriorate enough to influence their asset allocation significantly.

The Treasuries market was tense before the data as a weak jobs report would send yields sharply lower. Ten-year Treasury bond yields slipped 3 basis points to 4.265% on Thursday, while two-year yields (US2YT=RR) eased 2 bps to 3.772%.

In commodities markets, oil prices were lower after jumping 3% overnight as Iran suspended cooperation with the U.N. nuclear watchdog.

Brent crude futures slipped 0.8% to $68.64 per barrel, while U.S. crude was 0.7% lower on the day. (O/R) Gold prices (=XAU) also dipped, easing 0.1% to $3,352 an ounce. (GOL/)

data.title: Market Chatter: BlackRock Reportedly Mulls Sale of Stake in Leasing Rights to Saudi Aramco's Gas Pipeline Network
data.description:
Saudi Arabian Oil Co. (SASE:2222) d/b/a Saudi Aramco, is in talks to buy back BlackRock's stake in the leasing rights for its natural gas pipeline network.

The US-based investor will consider other options for the asset if talks with the oil giant fall through, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter. A final decision is yet to be made.

The stake is expected to be worth billions of dollars, according to the news outlet. A consortium led by BlackRock acquired the 49% stake for $15.5 billion in 2021.

Saudi Aramco and BlackRock did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' requests for comment.

Disclaimer

data.title: Eurozone Current Account Surplus Widens in Q1
data.description:
The euro area recorded a current account surplus of 366 billion euros in the first quarter of 2025, up from 319 billion euros in the prior-year period, data from the European Central Bank showed Thursday.

The reading reflects larger surpluses for goods and services, a lower surplus for primary income, and a growing secondary income deficit.

data.title: World Co. Retail Sales Down by 2% in June
data.description:
World Co domestic retail sales decreased by 2.2% in June, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Store sales fell 2.5%, while e-commerce sales slipped 1.2%, said the apparel company. Same-store sales declined by 3.5%.

The retailer decreased its retail stores by two to 2,023 in June.

data.title: Asian Star Anchor Chain's Unit Subscribes to Wealth Management Product for 30 Million Yuan
data.description:
Asian Star Anchor Chain's unit, Asian Star (Zhenjiang) Mooring Chain, subscribed to a broker wealth management product for 30.1 million yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Thursday.

The product, Xin Ying Stable (Beginning of Quarter) No. 3 FOF Plan, will have a term of 150 days.

The Chinese anchor chain manufacturer subscribed to the product from CITIC Securities Asset Management.

Asian Star's shares closed over 4% lower.

data.title: Volvo Cars Signs Contract to Produce Polestar 7 Electric SUVs in Slovakia
data.description:
Volvo Car AB (VOLCAR-B.ST), d/b/a Volvo Cars, signed a nonbinding agreement to develop and produce Swedish automotive manufacturer Polestar's new electric compact SUV, Polestar 7, at its plant in Kosice, Slovakia.

The Swedish automaker said Thursday that the new model is expected to be launched in 2028 and will be the second vehicle to be built at the facility, subject to final agreement.

Meanwhile, Polestar 7 will share its technology base with future Volvo models, benefiting from component sharing, next-gen battery tech, and over-the-air software upgrades.

data.title: Singapore's Straits Times Index Closes at Record-High — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.2% higher at 4019.57, hitting a record-high. The benchmark index crossed the 4000 mark for the first time in March. Maybank Research analysts expect capital rotations to accelerate amid uncertainties regarding U.S. trade policies. "Singapore stands to benefit given strong policy certainty, and a government with a clear mandate to cushion extreme outcomes," they said in a recent note. Among advancers, DFI Retail Group rose 5.0%, City Developments climbed 2.2% and Hongkong Land was up 1.7%. Meanwhile, UOL Group fell 1.2% and Keppel DC REIT lost 0.85%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

Result of Annual General Meeting

Keystone Law Group plc (AIM: KEYS), the premier tech-enabled platform law firm, announces that at the Company's Annual General Meeting held on 3 July 2025, all resolutions put to shareholders were passed unanimously on a show of hands.

The proxy figures for the resolutions will be made available on the Company's website later today.

Notes to editors

Keystone (AIM: KEYS) is a highly scalable, premier tech-enabled platform law firm. Ranked within the UK Top 100 law firms, providing conventional legal services in a £12bn addressable market through its differentiated platform model which has three defining characteristics:

·      Lawyers have freedom, flexibility and autonomy, and are paid up to 75% of what they bill.

·      Lawyers determine how, when and where they work, in contrast to the conventional law firm model.

·      Lawyers are provided full infrastructure and support via its central office team, bespoke user-friendly IT platform, and network of colleagues and events.

Keystone is a full-service law firm, with 20 service areas and more than 50 industry sectors delivered by over 450 high calibre self-employed Principal lawyers who work from their own offices.
